params.name: Nightingale Medal
params.description:
On Mar 24, 1912 AD, the world's first Nightingall Medal was awarded.
In the 19th century, Nightingale and her team went to the front lines to raise the standard of nursing care in field hospitals. Within a few months, she reduced the mortality rate of the wounded from 40% to 2%. The soldiers called her "Goddess with the Lamp" because she always patrolled the ward with a lamp. She served the patients with a spirit of humanity, fraternity, and dedication. Nightingale established nursing schools …
